title: primula denticulata subsp. harsukhii
intro: Primula denticulata subsp. harsukhii is a subspecies of the primrose plant native to the Himalayas, known for its delicate, white or yellow flowers and aromatic foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Primula denticulata subsp. harsukhii include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues such as cough and cold, and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to treat various health conditions.
